title: The Non Fat Yogurt

jokes.text: I had glasses when I was 10. Anybody beat that? Anybody got them younger? -Seven. -Seven? Two?
jokes.setup: Jerry asks who got glasses younger than 10
jokes.punchline: Audience members claim progressively younger ages, ending with absurd 'two'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor with good escalation structure

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Who got glasses before they were ten?

dialogue.speaker: Audience Member 1
dialogue.line: Eight!

dialogue.speaker: Audience Member 2
dialogue.line: Five!

dialogue.speaker: Audience Member 3
dialogue.line: Three!

dialogue.speaker: Audience Member 4
dialogue.line: Two!

jokes.text: Anybody born with glasses? Come out... Actually come out of the birth canal and go: 'That was a hell of a delivery, I'll tell you that. Can I clean these? Does anybody have one of those little cloths? I was just born. They're a little... A little smudgy.'
jokes.setup: Jerry escalates the glasses age question to birth
jokes.punchline: Baby delivering sophisticated commentary on delivery and asking for lens cloth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation with perfect character voice for a newborn. The specificity of 'little cloths' and 'smudgy' sells the bit completely.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So when were you born?

dialogue.speaker: Baby
dialogue.line: Well, I came out of the womb, and the first thing I noticed was the lighting was terrible. Very harsh. And these hands kept coming at my face. I said, 'Personal space, people.' Then they handed me to my mother, and I said, 'Nice try, but you're not the real thing.' Then they put these drops in my eyes, and I said, 'What is this, some kind of joke? Do you have a lens cloth?'

jokes.text: They put real blueberries in this. What kind did you get? Coffee. They grind up the coffee beans and put it in!
jokes.setup: Discussion of yogurt ingredients
jokes.punchline: Elaine's amazement at obvious fact that coffee yogurt contains coffee beans
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Elaine's enthusiasm for obvious details

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So what's in this coffee yogurt?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Coffee beans.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Coffee beans? How did they get coffee beans in yogurt?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I don't know, they just... put them in there.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, that's what makes it coffee yogurt.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh my God, I never realized that!

jokes.text: If you wanna taste mine, you don't have to offer me some of yours.
jokes.setup: Jerry offers to let Elaine taste his yogurt after she wants to taste his
jokes.punchline: Elaine points out Jerry's transparent reciprocity manipulation
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about social exchange dynamics, very economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Here, taste it.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Oh, you're good. You let me taste your yogurt so that later when you want to taste my dessert, I'll feel obligated to let you. It's a yogurt quid pro quo.

jokes.text: This is so f*****g good! Jerry! Oh, I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Elaine enthusiastically praising yogurt
jokes.punchline: She accidentally curses in front of a child, creating awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's cursing theme, good cringe moment

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: This yogurt is amazing! It's so creamy and delicious, I could eat this every single day!

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Oh for f—! I mean... hello there, little one.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Elaine!

jokes.text: This guy from my old neighborhood, Lloyd Braun. He's a big adviser to Mayor Dinkins. He thinks he's so cool.
jokes.setup: George sees someone he knows
jokes.punchline: George's immediate jealous dismissal and need to diminish Lloyd's importance
jokes.explanation: Perfect George jealousy and insecurity, establishes character dynamics

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Lloyd Braun! Hey, how are ya?

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: George! Great to see you!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah... so what are you up to these days?

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm doing really well. I just got promoted at work.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Promoted? That's... that's nice. I mean, it's not that big of a deal. Promotions happen all the time. I could get promoted tomorrow if I wanted to. I just don't want to.

jokes.text: Yeah, there was a fire in my apartment. A fire. There's a lot of major chicks in this place, huh?
jokes.setup: Lloyd asks about George living at home
jokes.punchline: George gives embarrassing honest answer then immediately changes subject awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Classic George oversharing followed by desperate subject change, very in character

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: You still living at home?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: My mother, she makes my breakfast, my lunch... she irons my clothes. I'm very happy.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: So, you like the Yankees?

jokes.text: Something wrong with your arm? Yeah, actually. I bumped my elbow on a desk and injured something... and now it sort of moves involuntarily.
jokes.setup: Lloyd notices George's arm movement
jokes.punchline: George has to invent elaborate lie to explain fake spasm
jokes.explanation: Introduces the arm subplot perfectly - George trapped by his own deception

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: What's wrong with your arm?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: My arm? Nothing's wrong with my arm.

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: You keep jerking it like that.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Oh, that. Yeah, it's a condition. Very rare. The doctors don't even have a name for it yet.

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: They're calling it George Costanza Syndrome. It comes and goes. Very unpredictable.

jokes.text: That's a bitch, huh?
jokes.setup: George explains complex medical condition
jokes.punchline: Lloyd's casual, dismissive response to what sounds serious
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery, economical and character-revealing

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I have this condition where my brain is getting smaller.

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, a lot of people have that.

jokes.text: Boy, he is really cute. He's a jerk. He's gone, George.
jokes.setup: Elaine comments on Lloyd's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: George immediately insults Lloyd, Jerry points out he's already left
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing George's reflexive jealousy and Jerry's observation

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Lloyd is so attractive.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Are you kidding? Look at him! He's a mess!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: George, he left five minutes ago.

jokes.text: I did this thing on the Ottoman Empire: What, was this a whole empire based on putting your feet up?
jokes.setup: Jerry describing his new comedy material
jokes.punchline: Ottoman Empire/ottoman furniture connection
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with good wordplay structure

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I was thinking about the Ottoman Empire, right? And I realized - nobody ever talks about Ottoman furniture. You know what I mean? It's an empire, but we only know it from a piece of furniture. That's all we got out of it. No other empire has that problem. You don't sit on a 'Roman Empire.' You don't put your feet up on a 'British Empire.' But the Ottomans? That's what we remember them by. Four hundred years of history, and we're like, 'Yeah, but did they make a good ottoman?'

jokes.text: I was having lunch and I bit down on the fork. It's hard to believe with so much biting experience... a person could still make a mistake like that.
jokes.setup: Elaine hurt her mouth
jokes.punchline: Treating biting as a skill you should master with experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on everyday mishap - treating basic human function as expertise

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I bit my mouth.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: How do you bite your own mouth?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I was eating and I wasn't paying attention.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, you've been eating your whole life. You'd think you'd be better at it by now.

jokes.text: You're getting heavy. What? You look like you put on 5... 10 pounds.
jokes.setup: Kramer looking at Jerry
jokes.punchline: Kramer's tactless weight observation with escalating estimate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer tactlessness, sets up weight gain mystery

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: You know, you're getting a little thick around the middle.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'd say you've put on... what, fifteen, twenty pounds?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I have not!

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: No no, maybe thirty. Thirty-five tops.

jokes.text: I'll tell you something else... you're looking a little chunky yourself. Me? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Kramer already insulted Jerry's weight
jokes.punchline: He continues the tactless observations with Elaine
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Kramer's tactlessness, very in character

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: You know, you really should do something about that hair. It's not doing you any favors.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Excuse me?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, a little conditioner wouldn't kill you.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I've gained 7 pounds! I've gained 8! I told you.
jokes.setup: They check the scale after Kramer's weight comments
jokes.punchline: Both have gained significant weight, Kramer vindicated
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to weight setup, drives main plot forward

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Okay, we're getting on the scale. Right now.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I don't want to get on the scale.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Come on, get on the scale.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I've gained eight pounds!

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I've gained seven!

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I told you! I told you people were getting fatter!

jokes.text: Maybe it's the Oreos. I don't eat Oreos. You don't eat Oreos? The way you break them open... You're practically having sex with them.
jokes.setup: Looking for cause of weight gain, Jerry denies eating Oreos
jokes.punchline: Elaine's vivid description of Jerry's Oreo eating technique
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific imagery that's both absurd and relatable. Great character observation.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Oh, come on! I've seen you! You sit there with a glass of milk, you twist it open, you scrape the filling out with your teeth, and you throw the cookie part over your shoulder!

jokes.text: What about me? You? You're getting old.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks about her own weight gain cause
jokes.punchline: Kramer's brutal, matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Devastatingly economical and tactless - perfect Kramer bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Why do you think I gained weight?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: You eat too much.

jokes.text: Maybe your yogurt isn't so nonfat. Oh, guess again, tubby.
jokes.setup: Elaine suggests yogurt might have fat
jokes.punchline: Kramer dismisses her with casual insult
jokes.explanation: Good character beat, Kramer's casual cruelty while defending his investment

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: The yogurt has fat in it.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Oh, you don't know what you're talking about.

jokes.text: Hey, I'll tell you what, chubs. If that yogurt has fat in it, I will put myself on an all-yogurt diet... for a week.
jokes.setup: Kramer confident yogurt is fat-free
jokes.punchline: Makes bet that would backfire spectacularly if he's wrong
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup for later payoff, very confident Kramer

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: It's non-fat yogurt. I'm telling you, it's non-fat.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: How do you know?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I know. I can tell by the way it feels.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You can tell by the way it feels?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Yeah, non-fat has a different... viscosity.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Viscosity?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I'm so confident, I'll bet you fifty bucks it's non-fat.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You're on.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: If I'm wrong, I'll never eat yogurt again. Ever.

jokes.text: Well, let's start the insanity. Giddyup.
jokes.setup: They're about to test the yogurt
jokes.punchline: Kramer's enthusiastic 'Giddyup' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer catchphrase moment, pure character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Giddyup!

jokes.text: Tap-dancing. Anyone can tap-dance. It's those shoes. Are you kidding? They practice for years, those people.
jokes.setup: Discussion of tap dancing skill
jokes.punchline: Frank's dismissive theory vs Estelle's defense
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's reductive thinking vs Estelle's reasonableness

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Tap dancing? That's not a skill. Any idiot can bang their feet on the ground.

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: Frank, that's not true. It takes coordination and rhythm.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Coordination? Estelle, I've seen you walk to the kitchen. You nearly took out the china cabinet last week.

jokes.text: Tommy Tune is very tall. That helps. It makes him lankier.
jokes.setup: Continuing tap dance discussion
jokes.punchline: Frank's odd logic about height helping with dancing
jokes.explanation: Frank's weird logic presented matter-of-factly, good character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Frank Costanza
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about this tap dancing thing. The taller you are, the better you tap dance.

dialogue.speaker: George Costanza
dialogue.line: Dad, that makes no sense.

dialogue.speaker: Frank Costanza
dialogue.line: Why not? More leg to work with! It's simple physics!

jokes.text: What's the matter with you?
jokes.setup: Lloyd mentions seeing George, George presumably does the arm thing
jokes.punchline: Estelle's confused reaction to seeing the fake spasm
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat, Estelle's genuine confusion at George's performance

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: What is that? What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Nothing, nothing, it's just a little thing I do.

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: Well, stop it. You look like you're having a stroke.

jokes.text: Your arm moves like this? I've never seen your arm move like this. Me either. Well, it comes and goes.
jokes.setup: Parents notice George's fake condition
jokes.punchline: George has to maintain lie to increasingly confused parents
jokes.explanation: Great cringe escalation as George's lie gets more complicated with witnesses

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: George, why are you walking like that?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I have a bad back. I threw it out at work.

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: You never told us about this!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: It just happened. It's very serious.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Well, you should see a doctor. I know a guy.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: No, no doctors. I'm handling it my own way.

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: George, you're sweating. Are you in pain right now?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Yes! Yes, terrible pain. Especially when I... when I bend like this.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: But you just bent like that five minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: That was before it got worse.

jokes.text: You discussed George with the mayor of New York? Dinkins was talking about you. He was discussing you!
jokes.setup: Learning Mayor Dinkins knows about George
jokes.punchline: Frank's amazement and pride building to peak excitement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank Costanza escalation and pride in his son's brush with fame

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The Mayor knows who you are?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Mayor Dinkins knows about you!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: This is incredible! My son, the Mayor knows about him!

jokes.text: Every word out of my son's mouth now is f**k, f**k, f**k. You know what he said to me five minutes ago? 'Where's my f*****g cupcake?'
jokes.setup: Mary complaining about her son's new cursing habit
jokes.punchline: Specific example of innocent request made profane
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing consequences of earlier setup, innocent request made hilariously inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Mary
dialogue.line: My son, he's picked up this terrible habit. He curses at everything.

dialogue.speaker: Mary
dialogue.line: Yesterday I asked him to pass the salt. You know what he said?

dialogue.speaker: Mary's Son
dialogue.line: Pass the f***ing salt!

dialogue.speaker: Mary
dialogue.line: The salt! Who curses at salt?

jokes.text: No f*****g way.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks if yogurt has fat while Mary complains about cursing
jokes.punchline: Mary unconsciously uses profanity while discussing the cursing problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment - Mary unconsciously demonstrates the very problem she's complaining about. Excellent economy and structure.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Does this yogurt have fat in it?

dialogue.speaker: Mary
dialogue.line: Well, you know, I wish you wouldn't use that kind of language around me. I find it very offensive.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What language? I didn't say anything.

dialogue.speaker: Mary
dialogue.line: Well, I don't give a damn what you said, just watch your f***ing mouth!

jokes.text: I wouldn't hear of it. I said, 'Nice try, granny' and I sent her to the back of the line.
jokes.setup: Newman presumably describing standing up to someone
jokes.punchline: Newman's cruel dismissal of an elderly person over yogurt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Newman pettiness and cruelty, very in character

dialogue.speaker: Newman
dialogue.line: I said to him, 'I don't care how old you are. You're not getting my yogurt!'

jokes.text: I've been waiting for something like this my whole life, and it's finally here!
jokes.setup: Newman talking about nonfat yogurt
jokes.punchline: Treating yogurt discovery as life's greatest achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Newman food obsession, absurdly dramatic over yogurt

dialogue.speaker: Newman
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, this nonfat yogurt is the greatest thing that's ever happened to me. I feel like I've discovered something profound, like I'm making a real contribution to society. This yogurt... it's changed my life.

jokes.text: Hey, fatso, I got a 90 in biology.
jokes.setup: Arguing about melted yogurt affecting test results
jokes.punchline: Kramer's juvenile insult combined with academic credential
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of childish insult with academic pride, very Kramer

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Oh, you're a real smart guy! You got your doctorate from a Froyo cup!

jokes.text: This is Mr. Giuliani's blood. We're doing a cholesterol workup on it.
jokes.setup: Casual mention at lab
jokes.punchline: Sets up major plot point about Giuliani's test results
jokes.explanation: Important plot setup that pays off later, subtle foreshadowing

jokes.text: You can't take that chemist out. Why not? Because she's like the jury. She's gotta be sequestered.
jokes.setup: Jerry wants to ask out the lab technician
jokes.punchline: Kramer applies legal trial logic to yogurt testing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic connecting dating ethics to jury sequestration, very Kramer reasoning

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I'm going to ask out the lab technician.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: You can't just ask her out. You need to establish a pattern of behavior first.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: It's like a trial. You need evidence, precedent. You can't just spring it on her.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: This isn't a courtroom, Kramer.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Everything's a courtroom. Even yogurt testing. You think they just taste it once and make a decision? No! They do it multiple times, build a case.

jokes.text: It smells. Smells bad. Smells really bad. That's enough. What? With the smells.
jokes.setup: Kramer commenting on something smelling
jokes.punchline: Jerry's exasperation with Kramer's repetitive complaints
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and Jerry's characteristic exasperation with repetition

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: What is that smell?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What smell? There's no smell.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: That smell! It's everywhere!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Kramer, there is no smell. You're imagining things.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I'm not imagining it! Don't you smell that?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: No! And you know what? I don't care anymore!

jokes.text: My whole life is a lie.
jokes.setup: Discussing having to maintain the arm lie
jokes.punchline: George's dramatic but matter-of-fact assessment of his existence
jokes.explanation: Perfect George line - simultaneously dramatic and casual about his fundamental dishonesty. Incredibly economical and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: It's not a lie if you believe it.

jokes.text: What if we get married? We'll always have that between us. Already you're marrying this guy?
jokes.setup: Elaine doesn't want to lie about George's arm
jokes.punchline: She immediately escalates to marriage concerns, George's disbelief
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Elaine's romantic optimism vs George's cynicism

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I'm not going to lie about your arm.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: What? Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Because it's wrong, George. If I lie about your arm, what's next? Am I going to lie about other things? And then one day we're married and I'm lying about everything!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: We're not getting married!

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I know, but if we were!

jokes.text: Of course. He's a funny f****r.
jokes.setup: Child meeting Jerry
jokes.punchline: Kid casually uses profanity in compliment
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing continued cursing problem, cringe-worthy but funny

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: Hey, you're a really funny guy. That's some good shit.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Thanks... I think.

jokes.text: He tells him what soap to use.
jokes.setup: Discussing Lloyd's advisory role with mayor
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and trivial example of political advice
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about the mundane nature of political advising

jokes.text: What the f**k are you doing, you little piece of s**t?!
jokes.setup: Jerry just explained cursing is bad
jokes.punchline: Kid immediately launches into elaborate profanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic timing - immediate contradiction of Jerry's anti-cursing lesson with escalated profanity

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: Are you kidding me? That's the best part!

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: *launches into elaborate string of profanities*

jokes.text: One of my campaign themes would be... that everybody should wear nametags to make the city friendlier. Nametags, huh? Everybody would know everybody. It would be like a small town.
jokes.setup: Elaine suggests campaign idea to Lloyd
jokes.punchline: Absurdly naive solution to urban alienation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly naive political idea that sets up Lloyd's downfall, very Elaine-like optimism

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You know what the problem is with the city? People are so alienated, so disconnected from each other.

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: What do you suggest?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Well, what if everyone just... smiled at each other more?

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: That's your solution?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Yeah! Just smiling. That would solve everything. Urban alienation: gone.

jokes.text: Then we went to his apartment, and I sat on one of his chairs... and it broke. And he says, 'Boy, you're a lot of woman.'
jokes.setup: Elaine explaining why Lloyd thinks she's fat
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing furniture incident and Lloyd's tactless comment
jokes.explanation: Great cringe escalation with perfect awkward euphemism from Lloyd

jokes.text: So people can go around... saying hello to one another. Oh, I see. So you go, 'Hey, you know who I saw wilding today? Herb!'
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining nametag policy benefits
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dark joke about identifying criminals by their nametags
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of naive policy with dark realistic consequence, perfect Jerry cynicism

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: See, with nametags, you know who you're dealing with. It's all very civilized.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Yeah, except now the serial killers know our names too.

jokes.text: Fat!
jokes.setup: Waiting for yogurt test results
jokes.punchline: One-word confirmation that validates all their suspicions
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical payoff to episode-long setup, maximum impact with minimum words

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Positive.

jokes.text: Obviously a very sick person. A very immature person. A person who has no regard for wasting other people's valuable time.
jokes.setup: Doctor diagnosing George as faker
jokes.punchline: Doctor's escalating condemnation of George's character
jokes.explanation: Good escalating condemnation, professional but brutal dismissal of George

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: You're not sick. You're a hypochondriac.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Actually, you're worse than a hypochondriac. You're a liar.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: And not just any liar. You're a petty, small-minded, conniving little man who wastes people's time with his ridiculous made-up ailments.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: I should report you to the medical board for wasting my time. But frankly, you're not worth the paperwork.

jokes.text: Rudy Giuliani, who underwent a physical last week... received some startling news today... when his cholesterol count turned out to be a whopping 375.
jokes.setup: News report about Giuliani's health
jokes.punchline: Extremely high cholesterol reading that affects election
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff connecting lab scene to major political consequence

dialogue.speaker: TV Reporter
dialogue.line: We're getting reports that Rudy Giuliani's cholesterol is at an extremely dangerous level. Doctors say if it gets any higher, he may have to drop out of the race.

jokes.text: Nonfat yogurt? Oh, my God. They got Giuliani, and he doesn't know it!
jokes.setup: Giuliani blames yogurt for his health problems
jokes.punchline: Kramer realizes they've inadvertently affected the election
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot convergence moment realizing their small action has huge political consequences

dialogue.speaker: Giuliani
dialogue.line: This yogurt has ruined my health! My digestion, my energy levels—everything!

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... if Giuliani's been sick this whole time, he couldn't campaign properly.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: We changed the entire election with a cup of yogurt!

jokes.text: What kind of an idiot thinks anybody would be interested in that idea? I don't think it's so bad. People should wear nametags. Everyone would be a lot friendlier. 'Hello, Sam.' 'How you doing, Joe?'
jokes.setup: Frank dismissing nametag idea
jokes.punchline: Estelle defends the very idea that got Lloyd fired
jokes.explanation: Good ironic defense of failed policy by sweet, naive Estelle vs cynical Frank

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: A nametag? Why would I wear a nametag? I'm Frank Costanza, everybody knows who I am.

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: But Frank, a nametag is professional. It shows you care about customer service. Lloyd wore one and look how well he did!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Lloyd? Lloyd got fired!

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: Yes, but it wasn't because of the nametag.

jokes.text: It was good! I was enjoying it. Had to interfere, couldn't leave well enough alone. I will get even with you for this. You can count on it.
jokes.setup: Newman confronting Jerry about yogurt scandal
jokes.punchline: Newman's dramatic threat over yogurt being exposed as fatty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Newman petty vengeance, treating yogurt scandal like a personal betrayal

dialogue.speaker: Newman
dialogue.line: You think you can just walk away from this? That yogurt was supposed to be non-fat!

dialogue.speaker: Newman
dialogue.line: I'm going to expose you, Jerry. I'm going to tell everyone what you did.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Newman, it was just yogurt.

dialogue.speaker: Newman
dialogue.line: JUST yogurt? This is about integrity! Your reputation will be ruined!

jokes.text: Apparently some blood spilled into Mr. Giuliani's test tube... causing his cholesterol count... to be 150 points higher than was initially reported.
jokes.setup: Reading news report about lab error
jokes.punchline: Lab mistake that actually helped Giuliani win election
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic payoff - their investigation accidentally caused the very thing that won Giuliani the election

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: A lab mistake that actually helped Giuliani win the election?

jokes.text: Thanks for ruining my daddy's business, you fat f**k.
jokes.setup: Kid confronting Jerry about yogurt business
jokes.punchline: Child's elaborate profanity while blaming Jerry for family business troubles
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback combining cursing theme with business consequences, kid's sophisticated cursing

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: You ruined my family's yogurt business, you son of a bitch! My father lost everything because of you, you bastard! Now my mother's working three jobs and my sister had to drop out of school. I hope you're happy, you goddamn son of a bitch!

jokes.text: There's nothing more fun than cursing when you're a kid. I mean, it's like getting the keys to the car. You're doing something you're not supposed to do.
jokes.setup: Jerry's closing standup about childhood cursing
jokes.punchline: Comparing cursing to getting car keys - both forbidden adult privileges
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational analogy that perfectly captures childhood psychology of transgression

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Cursing as a kid is like getting car keys. You know your parents have them, you see them use them all the time, but you can't have them. And when you finally get them, it's the best thing in the world.

jokes.text: And there's nothing less fun than when you're an adult... and having to use those wholesome curses: Fudge! Sugar! Consarnit!
jokes.setup: Contrast with adult experience
jokes.punchline: List of ridiculous substitute curse words adults use
jokes.explanation: Good observational escalation with specific funny examples of substitute curses

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You know what I've realized? As a kid, you can say any curse word you want. But as an adult, you can't. So what do we do? We come up with these ridiculous substitute words. 'What the frick?' 'Son of a biscuit!' 'Dag nabbit!' We sound insane. We're grown men yelling 'Shoot!' at traffic.

jokes.text: What the hell is 'consarnit' anyway? You stub your toe and say, 'Consarnit'... you might as well say, 'Yippee!'
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning the substitute curse word
jokes.punchline: Comparing ineffective curse to celebration, showing its uselessness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic about the complete inadequacy of substitute curses for expressing pain

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What's the deal with 'diddly'? It's not even a real curse word. You say 'diddly' and nobody knows what you mean. It's like saying 'yippee' - it's a celebration!

jokes.text: If you've been to a foreign country, the first thing you learn... are the curse words. Right? You travel halfway around the world to experience some exotic civilization... the first thing you ask is, 'How do they say doodie here?'
jokes.setup: Jerry talking about traveling and language learning
jokes.punchline: Reducing cultural exploration to learning bathroom words
jokes.explanation: Perfect observational truth about human nature - we prioritize the taboo over the educational when traveling

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You know what I've noticed about traveling? Everyone says you should learn the language. 'Learn the language, Jerry, it shows respect.' So I try. I get a phrase book. You know what I learn first? 'Where is the bathroom?' That's it. That's my cultural exploration. I'm reducing an entire civilization to their plumbing infrastructure. I don't know their history, their art, their literature. But I can tell you exactly where to take a dump in seventeen different languages.

tmdb_overview: Jerry and Elaine's suspicion about "nonfat" yogurt figures into New York City's mayoral race.
insight: Escalation-driven episode hits 53 jokes across 22 minutes but lands just below elite tier.
